context.pr_body:
previously Florence2 had flash attention as a requirement due to an error in *transformers* (issue with packages Forge uses rather than in Forge itself) (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/Florence-2-base/discussions/4#6673ffb9436907f83a8aaf2d); the workaround was to patch the imports to remove it (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/phi-1_5/discussions/72#65a0339f64520347aa3d703c).
At some point, an update has made this patch unnecessary. And turned it into an error.

This PR simply removes the patch.

Now the Space loads again; tested a couple of functions as working correctly too.

#2299

agents.system_prompt: You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README # Stable Diffusion WebUI Forge Stable Diffusion WebUI Forge is a platform on top of [Stable Diffusion WebUI](https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui) (based on [Gradio](https://www.gradio.app/) ) to make development easier, optimize resource management, speed up inference, and study experimental features. The name "Forge" is inspired from "Minecraft Forge". This project is aimed at becoming SD WebUI's Forge. Forge is currently based on SD-WebUI 1.10.1 at [this commit](https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui/commit/82a973c04367123ae98bd9abdf80d9eda9b910e2). (Because original SD-WebUI is almost static now, Forge will sync with original WebUI every 90 days, or when important fixes.) News are moved to this link: [Click here to see the News section](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/blob/main/NEWS.md) # Quick List [Gradio 4 UI Must Read (TLDR: You need to use RIGHT MOUSE BUTTON to move canvas!)](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/853) [Flux Tutorial (BitsandBytes Models, NF4, "GPU Weight", "Offload Location", "Offload Method", etc)](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/981) [Flux Tutorial 2 (Seperated Full Models, GGUF, Technically Correct Comparison between GGUF and NF4, etc)](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/1050) [Forge Extension List and Extension Replacement List (Temporary)](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/1754) [How to make LoRAs more precise on low-bit models; How to Skip" Patching LoRAs"; How to only load LoRA one time rather than each generation; How to report LoRAs that do not work](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/1038) [Report Flux Performance Problems (TLDR: DO NOT set "GPU Weight" too high! Lower "GPU Weight" solves 99% problems!)](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/1181) [How to solve "Connection errored out" / "Press anykey to continue ..." / etc](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/1474) [(Save Flux BitsandBytes UNet/Checkpoint)](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/1224#discussioncomment-10384104) [LayerDiffuse Transparent Image Editing](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/854) [Tell us what is missing in ControlNet Integrated](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/932) [(Policy) Soft Advertisement Removal Policy](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/discussions/1286) (Flux BNB NF4 / GGUF Q8_0/Q5_0/Q5_1/Q4_0/Q4_1 are all natively supported with GPU weight slider and Quene/Async Swap toggle and swap location toggle. All Flux BNB NF4 / GGUF Q8_0/Q5_0/Q4_0 have LoRA support.) ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods
agents.input_prompt:
Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
fix for Florence-2 space

## Description
previously Florence2 had flash attention as a requirement due to an error in *transformers* (issue with packages Forge uses rather than in Forge itself) (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/Florence-2-base/discussions/4#6673ffb9436907f83a8aaf2d); the workaround was to patch the imports to remove it (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/phi-1_5/discussions/72#65a0339f64520347aa3d703c).
At some point, an update has made this patch unnecessary. And turned it into an error.

This PR simply removes the patch.

Now the Space loads again; tested a couple of functions as working correctly too.

#2299

### File Overview
This module implements a core model patching system. It provides utilities to manage, apply, replace, and export patches (including LoRA-related patches) to neural network models, encapsulated in the ModelPatcher class and related helper functions.

### Architecture
- Core components:

tool_calls.raw_result:
import torch
from backend import operations, memory_management
from backend.patcher.base import ModelPatcher

from transformers import modeling_utils


class DiffusersModelPatcher:
    def __init__(self, pipeline_class, dtype=torch.float16, *args, **kwargs):
        load_device = memory_management.get_torch_device()
        offload_device = torch.device("cpu")

        if not memory_management.should_use_fp16(device=load_device):
            dtype = torch.float32

        self.dtype = dtype

        with operations.using_forge_operations():
            with modeling_utils.no_init_weights():
                self.pipeline = pipeline_class.from_pretrained(*args, **kwargs)

        if hasattr(self.pipeline, 'unet'):
            if hasattr(self.pipeline.unet, 'set_attn_processor'):
                from diffusers.models.attention_processor import AttnProcessor2_0
                self.pipeline.unet.set_attn_processor(AttnProcessor2_0())
                print('Attention optimization applied to DiffusersModelPatcher')

        self.pipeline = self.pipeline.to(device=offload_device)

        if self.dtype == torch.float16:
            self.pipeline = self.pipeline.half()

        self.pipeline.eval()

        self.patcher = ModelPatcher(
            model=self.pipeline,
            load_device=load_device,
            offload_device=offload_device)

    def prepare_memory_before_sampling(self, batchsize, latent_width, latent_height):
        area = 2 * batchsize * latent_width * latent_height
        inference_memory = (((area * 0.6) / 0.9) + 1024) * (1024 * 1024)
        memory_management.load_models_gpu(
            models=[self.patcher],
            memory_required=inference_memory
        )

    def move_tensor_to_current_device(self, x):
        return x.to(device=self.patcher.current_device, dtype=self.dtype)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class PatchTransformerEncoder(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, patch_size=10, embedding_dim=128, num_heads=4, use_class_token=False):
        """ViT-like transformer block

        Args:
            in_channels (int): Input channels
            patch_size (int, optional): patch size. Defaults to 10.
            embedding_dim (int, optional): Embedding dimension in transformer model. Defaults to 128.
            num_heads (int, optional): number of attention heads. Defaults to 4.
            use_class_token (bool, optional): Whether to use extra token at the start for global accumulation (called as "class token"). Defaults to False.
        """
        super(PatchTransformerEncoder, self).__init__()
        self.use_class_token = use_class_token
        encoder_layers = nn.TransformerEncoderLayer(
            embedding_dim, num_heads, dim_feedforward=1024)
        self.transformer_encoder = nn.TransformerEncoder(
            encoder_layers, num_layers=4)  # takes shape S,N,E

        self.embedding_convPxP = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, embedding_dim,
                                           kernel_size=patch_size, stride=patch_size, padding=0)
        
    def positional_encoding_1d(self, sequence_length, batch_size, embedding_dim, device='cpu'):
        """Generate positional encodings

        Args:
            sequence_length (int): Sequence length
            embedding_dim (int): Embedding dimension

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor SBE: Positional encodings
        """
        position = torch.arange(
            0, sequence_length, dtype=torch.float32, device=device).unsqueeze(1)
        index = torch.arange(
            0, embedding_dim, 2, dtype=torch.float32, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
        div_term = torch.exp(index * (-torch.log(torch.tensor(10000.0, device=device)) / embedding_dim))
        pos_encoding = position * div_term
        pos_encoding = torch.cat([torch.sin(pos_encoding), torch.cos(pos_encoding)], dim=1)
        pos_encoding = pos_encoding.unsqueeze(1).repeat(1, batch_size, 1)
        return pos_encoding
        

    def forward(self, x):
        """Forward pass

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor - NCHW): Input feature tensor

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor - SNE: Transformer output embeddings. S - sequence length (=HW/patch_size^2), N - batch size, E - embedding dim
        """
        embeddings = self.embedding_convPxP(x).flatten(
            2)  # .shape = n,c,s = n, embedding_dim, s
        if self.use_class_token:
            # extra special token at start ?
            embeddings = nn.functional.pad(embeddings, (1, 0))
        
        # change to S,N,E format required by transformer
        embeddings = embeddings.permute(2, 0, 1)
        S, N, E = embeddings.shape
        embeddings = embeddings + self.positional_encoding_1d(S, N, E, device=embeddings.device)
        x = self.transformer_encoder(embeddings)  # .shape = S, N, E
        return x

### Architecture
- PatchTransformerEncoder is the main module that combines:
  - embedding_convPxP: a 2D convolution that extracts patch embeddings from the input image (kernel size and stride equal to patch_size).
  - A Transformer encoder stack (TransformerEncoderLayer with embedding_dim and num_heads, wrapped by TransformerEncoder with 4 layers).
  - Optional class token support via use_class_token, implemented by padding the embeddings and prepending a token in the sequence.
  - positional_encoding_1d: a sinusoidal 1D positional encoding added to the patch embeddings before feeding them into the transformer.
- The forward pass converts input to patch embeddings, optionally adds a class token, applies positional encodings, and runs through the transformer to produce outputs with shape (S, N, E) (sequence length, batch size, embedding dim).

### Functions & Classes
- PatchTransformerEncoder — description and importance:
  - A PyTorch neural network module that implements a ViT-like patch embedding + transformer-based encoding pipeline. Important as the core component used to produce transformer embeddings from image patches.
- PatchTransformerEncoder.__init__ — description and importance:
  - Initializes the module with:
    - in_channels: number of input channels
    - patch_size: patch (and stride) size used by the Conv2d to create patches
    - embedding_dim: embedding dimension E for the transformer
    - num_heads: number of attention heads in the transformer
    - use_class_token: whether to prepend a class token to the sequence
  - Builds embedding_convPxP (Conv2d) and the transformer stack (TransformerEncoderLayer and TransformerEncoder). Critical for configuring the patch-based representation and the transformer architecture.
- PatchTransformerEncoder.positional_encoding_1d — description and importance:
  - Generates sinusoidal positional encodings for a sequence of length sequence_length with embedding dimension embedding_dim, applied across the batch. Used to provide the transformer with positional information about patch order.
- PatchTransformerEncoder.forward — description and importance:
  - Implements the forward pass:
    - Applies embedding_convPxP to produce patch embeddings and flattens the spatial dimensions.
    - Optionally pads to prepend a class token if use_class_token is True.
    - Permutes to (S, N, E) to fit the transformer's expected input format.
    - Adds positional encodings and passes through the transformer_encoder.
  - Returns the transformer output with shape (S, N, E). This is the main computation path producing the final patch-embedded representations.

            use_class_token (bool, optional): Whether to use extra token at the start for global accumulation (called as "class token"). Defaults to False.
        """
        super(PatchTransformerEncoder, self).__init__()
        self.use_class_token = use_class_token
        encoder_layers = nn.TransformerEncoderLayer(
            embedding_dim, num_heads, dim_feedforward=1024)
        self.transformer_encoder = nn.TransformerEncoder(
            encoder_layers, num_layers=4)  # takes shape S,N,E

        self.embedding_convPxP = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, embedding_dim,
                                           kernel_size=patch_size, stride=patch_size, padding=0)
        
    def positional_encoding_1d(self, sequence_length, batch_size, embedding_dim, device='cpu'):
        """Generate positional encodings

        Args:
            sequence_length (int): Sequence length
            embedding_dim (int): Embedding dimension

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor SBE: Positional encodings
        """
        position = torch.arange(
            0, sequence_length, dtype=torch.float32, device=device).unsqueeze(1)
        index = torch.arange(
            0, embedding_dim, 2, dtype=torch.float32, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
        div_term = torch.exp(index * (-torch.log(torch.tensor(10000.0, device=device)) / embedding_dim))
        pos_encoding = position * div_term
        pos_encoding = torch.cat([torch.sin(pos_encoding), torch.cos(pos_encoding)], dim=1)
        pos_encoding = pos_encoding.unsqueeze(1).repeat(1, batch_size, 1)
        return pos_encoding
        

    def forward(self, x):
        """Forward pass

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor - NCHW): Input feature tensor

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor - SNE: Transformer output embeddings. S - sequence length (=HW/patch_size^2), N - batch size, E - embedding dim
        """
        embeddings = self.embedding_convPxP(x).flatten(
            2)  # .shape = n,c,s = n, embedding_dim, s
        if self.use_class_token:
            # extra special token at start ?
            embeddings = nn.functional.pad(embeddings, (1, 0))
        
        # change to S,N,E format required by transformer
        embeddings = embeddings.permute(2, 0, 1)
        S, N, E = embeddings.shape
        embeddings = embeddings + self.positional_encoding_1d(S, N, E, device=embeddings.device)
        x = self.transformer_encoder(embeddings)  # .shape = S, N, E
        return x

This file defines a lightweight ViT-like patch transformer encoder that converts image patches into transformer embeddings and processes them with a PyTorch Transformer. It does not implement Flash Attention or Florence patching; it relies on PyTorch’s built-in Transformer components.

### Architecture
- PatchTransformerEncoder is the main module that combines:
  - embedding_convPxP: a 2D convolution that extracts patch embeddings from the input image (kernel size and stride equal to patch_size).
  - A Transformer encoder stack (TransformerEncoderLayer with embedding_dim and num_heads, wrapped by TransformerEncoder with 4 layers).
  - Optional class token support via use_class_token, implemented by padding the embeddings and prepending a token in the sequence.
  - positional_encoding_1d: a sinusoidal 1D positional encoding added to the patch embeddings before feeding them into the transformer.
- The forward pass converts input to patch embeddings, optionally adds a class token, applies positional encodings, and runs through the transformer to produce outputs with shape (S, N, E) (sequence length, batch size, embedding dim).

### Functions & Classes
- PatchTransformerEncoder — description and importance:
  - A PyTorch neural network module that implements a ViT-like patch embedding + transformer-based encoding pipeline. Important as the core component used to produce transformer embeddings from image patches.
- PatchTransformerEncoder.__init__ — description and importance:
  - Initializes the module with:
    - in_channels: number of input channels
    - patch_size: patch (and stride) size used by the Conv2d to create patches
    - embedding_dim: embedding dimension E for the transformer
    - num_heads: number of attention heads in the transformer
    - use_class_token: whether to prepend a class token to the sequence
  - Builds embedding_convPxP (Conv2d) and the transformer stack (TransformerEncoderLayer and TransformerEncoder). Critical for configuring the patch-based representation and the transformer architecture.
- PatchTransformerEncoder.positional_encoding_1d — description and importance:
  - Generates sinusoidal positional encodings for a sequence of length sequence_length with embedding dimension embedding_dim, applied across the batch. Used to provide the transformer with positional information about patch order.
- PatchTransformerEncoder.forward — description and importance:
  - Implements the forward pass:
    - Applies embedding_convPxP to produce patch embeddings and flattens the spatial dimensions.
    - Optionally pads to prepend a class token if use_class_token is True.
    - Permutes to (S, N, E) to fit the transformer's expected input format.
    - Adds positional encodings and passes through the transformer_encoder.
  - Returns the transformer output with shape (S, N, E). This is the main computation path producing the final patch-embedded representations.

### File Overview
This module provides patching utilities used by the patch manager to make model loading, downloads, and UI/network checks more robust. It defines helpers to fix Gradio URL checks, wrap loader functions to detect corrupted files, and a central patch_all_basics routine that applies patches to external libraries.

### Architecture
- Small set of utility functions (gradio_url_ok_fix, build_loaded, always_show_tqdm, long_path_prefix) that prepare and guard common operations against failures.
- patch_all_basics(): a centralized patch orchestrator that:
  - Replaces tqdm behavior for progress visibility.
  - Patches HuggingFace’s file download to handle long paths.
  - Sets a stricter, quieter logger for transformers.
  - Monkey-patches HuggingFace’s internal download helper to respect long path prefixes.
  - Patches Gradio’s URL validity check.
  - Wraps safetensors.torch.load_file and torch.load with additional error handling to surface corrupted file issues and relocate corrupted files.
- The file relies on runtime monkeypatching to alter behavior of external libraries without modifying their source.

### Functions & Classes
- gradio_url_ok_fix(url: str) -> bool — Checks the given URL by issuing up to five HEAD requests and returns True if the response status code is 200, 401, or 302. Used to patch Gradio’s networking URL check to be more tolerant/robust. This is important for ensuring patch manager can reliably determine if a URL is usable in environments with network quirks.
- build_loaded(module, loader_name) — Wraps an existing loader function inside a module with a protective wrapper:
  - If the original loader isn’t already stored under loader_name + '_origin', it saves it.
  - Creates a new loader that calls the original, but on exception:
    - Scans the provided path-like arguments for strings that point to existing files.
    - If a file is found, moves it to a .corrupted backup and informs the user that re-downloading may fix it.
    - Raises a ValueError with a detailed explanation including any file paths touched.
  - Replaces module.loader_name with the new wrapper.
  - Returns the modified module state (via side effects).
  This is crucial for robust model loading: it detects and isolates corrupted files and provides actionable guidance rather than cryptic failures.
- always_show_tqdm(*args, **kwargs) — Forces tqdm to always display progress output by setting disable to False, removing any explicit name parameter, and invoking tqdm with the provided args. Ensures progress indicators remain visible in patch environments where stdout may be redirected.
- long_path_prefix(path: Path) -> Path — On Windows (nt), if the path does not already have the extended-length prefix (\\\\?\\) and the path doesn’t exist yet, returns a Path prefixed with \\\\?\\ to enable long-path support. Otherwise returns the original path. Supports operations involving very long file paths during downloads and loading.
- patch_all_basics() — The central patching routine that wires up multiple patches:
  - Imports logging and sets gradio/huggingface-related patches:
    - Sets file_download.tqdm = always_show_tqdm to ensure progress bars are visible.
    - Sets the transformers logger level to ERROR to reduce noisy logs.
  - Patches HuggingFace’s internal file download helper:
    - Stores the original _download_to_tmp_and_move as original_download_to_tmp_and_move.
    - Defines patched_download_to_tmp_and_move(incomplete_path, destination_path, url_to_download, proxies, headers, expected_size, filename, force_download) which applies long_path_prefix to incomplete and destination paths before delegating to the original function.
    - Replaces file_download._download_to_tmp_and_move with the patched version.
  - Patches Gradio’s URL check by assigning gradio.networking.url_ok = gradio_url_ok_fix.
  - Applies build_loaded to wrap safetensors.torch load_file with a protective loader name 'load_file', and to wrap torch.load with a protective loader name 'load'.
  - Returns nothing; the patches are applied via side effects (monkeypatching).

Note: The inner function patched_download_to_tmp_and_move is defined inside patch_all_basics with the exact signature and name, and is used to override the original downloader behavior.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This file directly supports the agent’s goal of patch management and Florence patch support by providing core, reusable patches that improve reliability when downloading and loading model components.
- It enables robust error handling for corrupted model files, ensures long-path issues on Windows are mitigated, and makes progress indicators consistently visible during patch operations.
- By patching Gradio’s URL checks and HuggingFace’s download logic, it lays the groundwork for stable operation in environments where network reliability or path length could otherwise cause failures in a patch workflow.
- The presence of pre-wrapped loaders for safetensors.torch and torch indicates a focus on safely loading model artifacts during patch application and management, which is central to Florence-like patch support.

import torch

from huggingface_guess import model_list
from backend.diffusion_engine.base import ForgeDiffusionEngine, ForgeObjects
from backend.patcher.clip import CLIP
from backend.patcher.vae import VAE
from backend.patcher.unet import UnetPatcher
from backend.text_processing.classic_engine import ClassicTextProcessingEngine
from backend.text_processing.t5_engine import T5TextProcessingEngine
from backend.args import dynamic_args
from backend.modules.k_prediction import PredictionFlux
from backend import memory_management


class Flux(ForgeDiffusionEngine):
    matched_guesses = [model_list.Flux, model_list.FluxSchnell]

    def __init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components):
        super().__init__(estimated_config, huggingface_components)
        self.is_inpaint = False

        clip = CLIP(
            model_dict={
                'clip_l': huggingface_components['text_encoder'],
                't5xxl': huggingface_components['text_encoder_2']
            },
            tokenizer_dict={
                'clip_l': huggingface_components['tokenizer'],
                't5xxl': huggingface_components['tokenizer_2']
            }
        )

        vae = VAE(model=huggingface_components['vae'])

        if 'schnell' in estimated_config.huggingface_repo.lower():
            k_predictor = PredictionFlux(
                mu=1.0
            )
        else:
            k_predictor = PredictionFlux(
                seq_len=4096,
                base_seq_len=256,
                max_seq_len=4096,
                base_shift=0.5,
                max_shift=1.15,
            )
            self.use_distilled_cfg_scale = True

        unet = UnetPatcher.from_model(
            model=huggingface_components['transformer'],
            diffusers_scheduler=None,
            k_predictor=k_predictor,
            config=estimated_config
        )

        self.text_processing_engine_l = ClassicTextProcessingEngine(
            text_encoder=clip.cond_stage_model.clip_l,
            tokenizer=clip.tokenizer.clip_l,
            embedding_dir=dynamic_args['embedding_dir'],
            embedding_key='clip_l',
            embedding_expected_shape=768,
            emphasis_name=dynamic_args['emphasis_name'],
            text_projection=False,
            minimal_clip_skip=1,
            clip_skip=1,
            return_pooled=True,
            final_layer_norm=True,
        )

        self.text_processing_engine_t5 = T5TextProcessingEngine(
            text_encoder=clip.cond_stage_model.t5xxl,
            tokenizer=clip.tokenizer.t5xxl,
            emphasis_name=dynamic_args['emphasis_name'],
        )

        self.forge_objects = ForgeObjects(unet=unet, clip=clip, vae=vae, clipvision=None)
        self.forge_objects_original = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()
        self.forge_objects_after_applying_lora = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()

    def set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip):
        self.text_processing_engine_l.clip_skip = clip_skip

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str]):
        memory_management.load_model_gpu(self.forge_objects.clip.patcher)
        cond_l, pooled_l = self.text_processing_engine_l(prompt)
        cond_t5 = self.text_processing_engine_t5(prompt)
        cond = dict(crossattn=cond_t5, vector=pooled_l)

        if self.use_distilled_cfg_scale:
            distilled_cfg_scale = getattr(prompt, 'distilled_cfg_scale', 3.5) or 3.5
            cond['guidance'] = torch.FloatTensor([distilled_cfg_scale] * len(prompt))
            print(f'Distilled CFG Scale: {distilled_cfg_scale}')
        else:
            print('Distilled CFG Scale will be ignored for Schnell')

        return cond

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt):
        token_count = len(self.text_processing_engine_t5.tokenize([prompt])[0])
        return token_count, max(255, token_count)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def encode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.encode(x.movedim(1, -1) * 0.5 + 0.5)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_in(sample)
        return sample.to(x)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def decode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_out(x)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.decode(sample).movedim(-1, 1) * 2.0 - 1.0
        return sample.to(x)

### File Overview
This file defines the Flux diffusion engine integration, wiring together patchers (CLIP, VAE, Unet), text processing engines, and a customized UNet for Flux model variants. It exposes conditioning, UI prompt length, and first-stage encode/decode helpers used during diffusion.

### Architecture
- Flux class (inherits ForgeDiffusionEngine) orchestrates model components for Flux/FluxSchnell variants.
- Patchers:
  - CLIP (clip patcher) built from huggingface components for text/image conditioning.
  - VAE (vae patcher) used for encoding/decoding latent representations.
  - UnetPatcher (from_model) to wrap/patch the UNet with a custom k_predictor.
- Text processing engines:
  - ClassicTextProcessingEngine (for the L-based text conditioning, using clip_l and tokenizer_l).
  - T5TextProcessingEngine (for the T5-based conditioning branch).
- ForgeObjects container aggregates model components (unet, clip, vae, clipvision=None) and supports shallow copies to manage variations (original vs. after applying LoRA).
- Memory and arguments:
  - memory_management.load_model_gpu ensures the patcher is loaded onto the GPU.
  - dynamic_args supplies embedding and emphasis configuration.
- Runtime behavior:
  - Chooses a k_predictor based on the model variant (Flux vs Flux Schnelle).
  - Provides conditioning via get_learned_conditioning, prompt-length checks, and first-stage encode/decode helpers.

### Functions & Classes
- Flux — description: Primary diffusion engine class for Flux models; coordinates patchers, text processing, and UNet patches to produce conditioning for image generation.

- Flux.__init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components) — description and importance:
  - Initializes the Flux diffusion engine, sets is_inpaint to False.
  - Builds CLIP patcher with text_encoder/text_encoder_2 and tokenizers.
  - Creates VAE patcher from provided VAE component.
  - Chooses k_predictor:
    - If "schnell" appears in estimated_config.huggingface_repo (case-insensitive), uses PredictionFlux(mu=1.0).
    - Otherwise uses a more feature-rich configuration (seq_len, base_seq_len, max_seq_len, base_shift, max_shift) and enables self.use_distilled_cfg_scale.
  - Creates UnetPatcher via from_model with the provided transformer model and k_predictor.
  - Instantiates text processing engines:
    - ClassicTextProcessingEngine with clip_l, tokenizer_l, embedding options, skip settings, and pooling/normalization flags.
    - T5TextProcessingEngine with t5xxl text encoder and tokenizer.
  - Assembles forge_objects = ForgeObjects(unet=unet, clip=clip, vae=vae, clipvision=None) and creates shallow copies for original and LoRA-applied variants.
  - Importance: Central setup of diffusion components and their configurations; determines whether to use distilled CFG scaling and how text conditioning is computed.

- Flux.set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip) — description and importance:
  - Sets the clip_skip parameter on the L-side text processing engine (self.text_processing_engine_l.clip_skip).
  - Importance: Adjusts prompt token processing/conditioning depth for the L-based path, enabling UI/user control over skip behavior.

- Flux.get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str]) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode() for efficient inference.
  - Loads the patcher onto the GPU: memory_management.load_model_gpu(self.forge_objects.clip.patcher).
  - Obtains L-based conditioning: cond_l and pooled_l via self.text_processing_engine_l(prompt).
  - Obtains T5-based conditioning: cond_t5 via self.text_processing_engine_t5(prompt).
  - Builds cond = dict(crossattn=cond_t5, vector=pooled_l).
  - If self.use_distilled_cfg_scale is True, adds a "guidance" key:
    - Reads distilled_cfg_scale from prompt (default 3.5 if missing) and creates a tensor [distilled_cfg_scale] * len(prompt).
    - Prints the distilled CFG scale value.
  - If not using distilled CFG scale, prints a message indicating it will be ignored for Schnell.
  - Returns cond dict for downstream diffusion steps.
  - Importance: Core method to assemble cross-attention and vector conditioning used by the UNet during denoising; also handles optional distilled CFG scaling.

- Flux.get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode().
  - Tokenizes the prompt using self.text_processing_engine_t5 and returns:
    - token_count: number of tokens.
    - max_required: max(255, token_count).
  - Importance: Provides UI-relevant prompt length bounds, ensuring prompts stay within practical limits for processing.

- Flux.encode_first_stage(self, x) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode().
  - Encodes the input latent x via the VAE patcher:
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.encode(x.movedim(1, -1) * 0.5 + 0.5)
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_in(sample)
  - Returns sample re-aligned to input device: sample.to(x)
  - Importance: Transforms latent representations into a suitable encoded form for the diffusion process.

- Flux.decode_first_stage(self, x) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode().
  - Decodes latent x via the VAE patcher:
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_out(x)
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.decode(sample).movedim(-1, 1) * 2.0 - 1.0
  - Returns sample on the original device: sample.to(x)
  - Importance: Converts model outputs back to image-like space for final image generation or visualization.

import torch

from huggingface_guess import model_list
from backend.diffusion_engine.base import ForgeDiffusionEngine, ForgeObjects
from backend.patcher.clip import CLIP
from backend.patcher.vae import VAE
from backend.patcher.unet import UnetPatcher
from backend.text_processing.classic_engine import ClassicTextProcessingEngine
from backend.text_processing.t5_engine import T5TextProcessingEngine
from backend.args import dynamic_args
from backend.modules.k_prediction import PredictionFlux
from backend import memory_management


class Flux(ForgeDiffusionEngine):
    matched_guesses = [model_list.Flux, model_list.FluxSchnell]

    def __init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components):
        super().__init__(estimated_config, huggingface_components)
        self.is_inpaint = False

        clip = CLIP(
            model_dict={
                'clip_l': huggingface_components['text_encoder'],
                't5xxl': huggingface_components['text_encoder_2']
            },
            tokenizer_dict={
                'clip_l': huggingface_components['tokenizer'],
                't5xxl': huggingface_components['tokenizer_2']
            }
        )

        vae = VAE(model=huggingface_components['vae'])

        if 'schnell' in estimated_config.huggingface_repo.lower():
            k_predictor = PredictionFlux(
                mu=1.0
            )
        else:
            k_predictor = PredictionFlux(
                seq_len=4096,
                base_seq_len=256,
                max_seq_len=4096,
                base_shift=0.5,
                max_shift=1.15,
            )
            self.use_distilled_cfg_scale = True

        unet = UnetPatcher.from_model(
            model=huggingface_components['transformer'],
            diffusers_scheduler=None,
            k_predictor=k_predictor,
            config=estimated_config
        )

        self.text_processing_engine_l = ClassicTextProcessingEngine(
            text_encoder=clip.cond_stage_model.clip_l,
            tokenizer=clip.tokenizer.clip_l,
            embedding_dir=dynamic_args['embedding_dir'],
            embedding_key='clip_l',
            embedding_expected_shape=768,
            emphasis_name=dynamic_args['emphasis_name'],
            text_projection=False,
            minimal_clip_skip=1,
            clip_skip=1,
            return_pooled=True,
            final_layer_norm=True,
        )

        self.text_processing_engine_t5 = T5TextProcessingEngine(
            text_encoder=clip.cond_stage_model.t5xxl,
            tokenizer=clip.tokenizer.t5xxl,
            emphasis_name=dynamic_args['emphasis_name'],
        )

        self.forge_objects = ForgeObjects(unet=unet, clip=clip, vae=vae, clipvision=None)
        self.forge_objects_original = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()
        self.forge_objects_after_applying_lora = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()

    def set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip):
        self.text_processing_engine_l.clip_skip = clip_skip

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str]):
        memory_management.load_model_gpu(self.forge_objects.clip.patcher)
        cond_l, pooled_l = self.text_processing_engine_l(prompt)
        cond_t5 = self.text_processing_engine_t5(prompt)
        cond = dict(crossattn=cond_t5, vector=pooled_l)

        if self.use_distilled_cfg_scale:
            distilled_cfg_scale = getattr(prompt, 'distilled_cfg_scale', 3.5) or 3.5
            cond['guidance'] = torch.FloatTensor([distilled_cfg_scale] * len(prompt))
            print(f'Distilled CFG Scale: {distilled_cfg_scale}')
        else:
            print('Distilled CFG Scale will be ignored for Schnell')

        return cond

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt):
        token_count = len(self.text_processing_engine_t5.tokenize([prompt])[0])
        return token_count, max(255, token_count)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def encode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.encode(x.movedim(1, -1) * 0.5 + 0.5)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_in(sample)
        return sample.to(x)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def decode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_out(x)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.decode(sample).movedim(-1, 1) * 2.0 - 1.0
        return sample.to(x)

### File Overview
This file defines the Flux diffusion engine integration, wiring together patchers (CLIP, VAE, Unet), text processing engines, and a customized UNet for Flux model variants. It exposes conditioning, UI prompt length, and first-stage encode/decode helpers used during diffusion.

### Architecture
- Flux class (inherits ForgeDiffusionEngine) orchestrates model components for Flux/FluxSchnell variants.
- Patchers:
  - CLIP (clip patcher) built from huggingface components for text/image conditioning.
  - VAE (vae patcher) used for encoding/decoding latent representations.
  - UnetPatcher (from_model) to wrap/patch the UNet with a custom k_predictor.
- Text processing engines:
  - ClassicTextProcessingEngine (for the L-based text conditioning, using clip_l and tokenizer_l).
  - T5TextProcessingEngine (for the T5-based conditioning branch).
- ForgeObjects container aggregates model components (unet, clip, vae, clipvision=None) and supports shallow copies to manage variations (original vs. after applying LoRA).
- Memory and arguments:
  - memory_management.load_model_gpu ensures the patcher is loaded onto the GPU.
  - dynamic_args supplies embedding and emphasis configuration.
- Runtime behavior:
  - Chooses a k_predictor based on the model variant (Flux vs Flux Schnelle).
  - Provides conditioning via get_learned_conditioning, prompt-length checks, and first-stage encode/decode helpers.

### Functions & Classes
- Flux — description: Primary diffusion engine class for Flux models; coordinates patchers, text processing, and UNet patches to produce conditioning for image generation.

- Flux.__init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components) — description and importance:
  - Initializes the Flux diffusion engine, sets is_inpaint to False.
  - Builds CLIP patcher with text_encoder/text_encoder_2 and tokenizers.
  - Creates VAE patcher from provided VAE component.
  - Chooses k_predictor:
    - If "schnell" appears in estimated_config.huggingface_repo (case-insensitive), uses PredictionFlux(mu=1.0).
    - Otherwise uses a more feature-rich configuration (seq_len, base_seq_len, max_seq_len, base_shift, max_shift) and enables self.use_distilled_cfg_scale.
  - Creates UnetPatcher via from_model with the provided transformer model and k_predictor.
  - Instantiates text processing engines:
    - ClassicTextProcessingEngine with clip_l, tokenizer_l, embedding options, skip settings, and pooling/normalization flags.
    - T5TextProcessingEngine with t5xxl text encoder and tokenizer.
  - Assembles forge_objects = ForgeObjects(unet=unet, clip=clip, vae=vae, clipvision=None) and creates shallow copies for original and LoRA-applied variants.
  - Importance: Central setup of diffusion components and their configurations; determines whether to use distilled CFG scaling and how text conditioning is computed.

- Flux.set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip) — description and importance:
  - Sets the clip_skip parameter on the L-side text processing engine (self.text_processing_engine_l.clip_skip).
  - Importance: Adjusts prompt token processing/conditioning depth for the L-based path, enabling UI/user control over skip behavior.

- Flux.get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str]) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode() for efficient inference.
  - Loads the patcher onto the GPU: memory_management.load_model_gpu(self.forge_objects.clip.patcher).
  - Obtains L-based conditioning: cond_l and pooled_l via self.text_processing_engine_l(prompt).
  - Obtains T5-based conditioning: cond_t5 via self.text_processing_engine_t5(prompt).
  - Builds cond = dict(crossattn=cond_t5, vector=pooled_l).
  - If self.use_distilled_cfg_scale is True, adds a "guidance" key:
    - Reads distilled_cfg_scale from prompt (default 3.5 if missing) and creates a tensor [distilled_cfg_scale] * len(prompt).
    - Prints the distilled CFG scale value.
  - If not using distilled CFG scale, prints a message indicating it will be ignored for Schnell.
  - Returns cond dict for downstream diffusion steps.
  - Importance: Core method to assemble cross-attention and vector conditioning used by the UNet during denoising; also handles optional distilled CFG scaling.

- Flux.get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode().
  - Tokenizes the prompt using self.text_processing_engine_t5 and returns:
    - token_count: number of tokens.
    - max_required: max(255, token_count).
  - Importance: Provides UI-relevant prompt length bounds, ensuring prompts stay within practical limits for processing.

- Flux.encode_first_stage(self, x) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode().
  - Encodes the input latent x via the VAE patcher:
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.encode(x.movedim(1, -1) * 0.5 + 0.5)
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_in(sample)
  - Returns sample re-aligned to input device: sample.to(x)
  - Importance: Transforms latent representations into a suitable encoded form for the diffusion process.

- Flux.decode_first_stage(self, x) — description and importance:
  - Decorated with @torch.inference_mode().
  - Decodes latent x via the VAE patcher:
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_out(x)
    - sample = self.forge_objects.vae.decode(sample).movedim(-1, 1) * 2.0 - 1.0
  - Returns sample on the original device: sample.to(x)
  - Importance: Converts model outputs back to image-like space for final image generation or visualization.

import torch
import safetensors.torch as sf

from backend import utils


class ForgeObjects:
    def __init__(self, unet, clip, vae, clipvision):
        self.unet = unet
        self.clip = clip
        self.vae = vae
        self.clipvision = clipvision

    def shallow_copy(self):
        return ForgeObjects(
            self.unet,
            self.clip,
            self.vae,
            self.clipvision
        )


class ForgeDiffusionEngine:
    matched_guesses = []

    def __init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components):
        self.model_config = estimated_config
        self.is_inpaint = estimated_config.inpaint_model()

        self.forge_objects = None
        self.forge_objects_original = None
        self.forge_objects_after_applying_lora = None

        self.current_lora_hash = str([])

        self.fix_for_webui_backward_compatibility()

    def set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip):
        pass

    def get_first_stage_encoding(self, x):
        return x  # legacy code, do not change

    def get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str]):
        pass

    def encode_first_stage(self, x):
        pass

    def decode_first_stage(self, x):
        pass

    def get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt):
        return 0, 75

    def is_webui_legacy_model(self):
        return self.is_sd1 or self.is_sd2 or self.is_sdxl or self.is_sd3

    def fix_for_webui_backward_compatibility(self):
        self.tiling_enabled = False
        self.first_stage_model = None
        self.cond_stage_model = None
        self.use_distilled_cfg_scale = False
        self.is_sd1 = False
        self.is_sd2 = False
        self.is_sdxl = False
        self.is_sd3 = False
        return

    def save_unet(self, filename):
        sd = utils.get_state_dict_after_quant(self.forge_objects.unet.model.diffusion_model)
        sf.save_file(sd, filename)
        return filename

    def save_checkpoint(self, filename):
        sd = {}
        sd.update(
            utils.get_state_dict_after_quant(self.forge_objects.unet.model.diffusion_model, prefix='model.diffusion_model.')
        )
        sd.update(
            utils.get_state_dict_after_quant(self.forge_objects.clip.cond_stage_model, prefix='text_encoders.')
        )
        sd.update(
            utils.get_state_dict_after_quant(self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model, prefix='vae.')
        )
        sf.save_file(sd, filename)
        return filename

### File Overview
This file defines a specialized diffusion engine class for Stable Diffusion 2.0 that wires together patched components (via UnetPatcher, CLIP/VAE patching) and a text-processing pipeline. It serves as an adapter to load, patch, and run SD2 models within the system, exposing conditioning, encoding/decoding, and UI-related utilities.

### Architecture
- The core is a subclass of ForgeDiffusionEngine, named StableDiffusion2, which binds together patched model components.
- Initialization (__init__) orchestrates:
  - A CLIP wrapper built from huggingface components.
  - A VAE wrapper built from huggingface components.
  - An UnetPatcher instantiated from a patched UNet, with a diffusers scheduler and a provided config.
  - A ClassicTextProcessingEngine configured to work with the patched CLIP and embedding settings.
  - A ForgeObjects container aggregating the patched components (unet, clip, vae) and placeholders for clipvision.
  - Clones of the ForgeObjects for restoration and LoRA-related modifications.
- The class exposes methods to obtain conditioning, UI prompt length statistics, and to encode/decode the first diffusion stage, all leveraging the patched components and memory management utilities.

### Functions & Classes
- StableDiffusion2 — description and importance
  - Role: Principal engine tying SD2 components together; identifies the SD2 variant via matched_guesses and exposes the patched pipeline through forge_objects.

import torch

from huggingface_guess import model_list
from backend.diffusion_engine.base import ForgeDiffusionEngine, ForgeObjects
from backend.patcher.clip import CLIP
from backend.patcher.vae import VAE
from backend.patcher.unet import UnetPatcher
from backend.text_processing.classic_engine import ClassicTextProcessingEngine
from backend.args import dynamic_args
from backend import memory_management


class StableDiffusion2(ForgeDiffusionEngine):
    matched_guesses = [model_list.SD20]

    def __init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components):
        super().__init__(estimated_config, huggingface_components)

        clip = CLIP(
            model_dict={
                'clip_h': huggingface_components['text_encoder']
            },
            tokenizer_dict={
                'clip_h': huggingface_components['tokenizer']
            }
        )

        vae = VAE(model=huggingface_components['vae'])

        unet = UnetPatcher.from_model(
            model=huggingface_components['unet'],
            diffusers_scheduler=huggingface_components['scheduler'],
            config=estimated_config
        )

        self.text_processing_engine = ClassicTextProcessingEngine(
            text_encoder=clip.cond_stage_model.clip_h,
            tokenizer=clip.tokenizer.clip_h,
            embedding_dir=dynamic_args['embedding_dir'],
            embedding_key='clip_h',
            embedding_expected_shape=1024,
            emphasis_name=dynamic_args['emphasis_name'],
            text_projection=False,
            minimal_clip_skip=1,
            clip_skip=1,
            return_pooled=False,
            final_layer_norm=True,
        )

        self.forge_objects = ForgeObjects(unet=unet, clip=clip, vae=vae, clipvision=None)
        self.forge_objects_original = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()
        self.forge_objects_after_applying_lora = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()

        # WebUI Legacy
        self.is_sd2 = True

    def set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip):
        self.text_processing_engine.clip_skip = clip_skip

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str]):
        memory_management.load_model_gpu(self.forge_objects.clip.patcher)
        cond = self.text_processing_engine(prompt)
        return cond

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt):
        _, token_count = self.text_processing_engine.process_texts([prompt])
        return token_count, self.text_processing_engine.get_target_prompt_token_count(token_count)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def encode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.encode(x.movedim(1, -1) * 0.5 + 0.5)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_in(sample)
        return sample.to(x)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def decode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_out(x)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.decode(sample).movedim(-1, 1) * 2.0 - 1.0
        return sample.to(x)

import torch

from huggingface_guess import model_list
from backend.diffusion_engine.base import ForgeDiffusionEngine, ForgeObjects
from backend.patcher.clip import CLIP
from backend.patcher.vae import VAE
from backend.patcher.unet import UnetPatcher
from backend.text_processing.classic_engine import ClassicTextProcessingEngine
from backend.args import dynamic_args
from backend import memory_management
from backend.nn.unet import Timestep


class StableDiffusionXL(ForgeDiffusionEngine):
    matched_guesses = [model_list.SDXL]

    def __init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components):
        super().__init__(estimated_config, huggingface_components)

        clip = CLIP(
            model_dict={
                'clip_l': huggingface_components['text_encoder'],
                'clip_g': huggingface_components['text_encoder_2']
            },
            tokenizer_dict={
                'clip_l': huggingface_components['tokenizer'],
                'clip_g': huggingface_components['tokenizer_2']
            }
        )

        vae = VAE(model=huggingface_components['vae'])

        unet = UnetPatcher.from_model(
            model=huggingface_components['unet'],
            diffusers_scheduler=huggingface_components['scheduler'],
            config=estimated_config
        )

        self.text_processing_engine_l = ClassicTextProcessingEngine(
            text_encoder=clip.cond_stage_model.clip_l,
            tokenizer=clip.tokenizer.clip_l,
            embedding_dir=dynamic_args['embedding_dir'],
            embedding_key='clip_l',
            embedding_expected_shape=2048,
            emphasis_name=dynamic_args['emphasis_name'],
            text_projection=False,
            minimal_clip_skip=2,
            clip_skip=2,
            return_pooled=False,
            final_layer_norm=False,
        )

        self.text_processing_engine_g = ClassicTextProcessingEngine(
            text_encoder=clip.cond_stage_model.clip_g,
            tokenizer=clip.tokenizer.clip_g,
            embedding_dir=dynamic_args['embedding_dir'],
            embedding_key='clip_g',
            embedding_expected_shape=2048,
            emphasis_name=dynamic_args['emphasis_name'],
            text_projection=True,
            minimal_clip_skip=2,
            clip_skip=2,
            return_pooled=True,
            final_layer_norm=False,
        )

        self.embedder = Timestep(256)

        self.forge_objects = ForgeObjects(unet=unet, clip=clip, vae=vae, clipvision=None)
        self.forge_objects_original = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()
        self.forge_objects_after_applying_lora = self.forge_objects.shallow_copy()

        # WebUI Legacy
        self.is_sdxl = True

    def set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip):
        self.text_processing_engine_l.clip_skip = clip_skip
        self.text_processing_engine_g.clip_skip = clip_skip

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str]):
        memory_management.load_model_gpu(self.forge_objects.clip.patcher)

        cond_l = self.text_processing_engine_l(prompt)
        cond_g, clip_pooled = self.text_processing_engine_g(prompt)

        width = getattr(prompt, 'width', 1024) or 1024
        height = getattr(prompt, 'height', 1024) or 1024
        is_negative_prompt = getattr(prompt, 'is_negative_prompt', False)

        crop_w = 0
        crop_h = 0
        target_width = width
        target_height = height

        out = [
            self.embedder(torch.Tensor([height])), self.embedder(torch.Tensor([width])),
            self.embedder(torch.Tensor([crop_h])), self.embedder(torch.Tensor([crop_w])),
            self.embedder(torch.Tensor([target_height])), self.embedder(torch.Tensor([target_width]))
        ]

        flat = torch.flatten(torch.cat(out)).unsqueeze(dim=0).repeat(clip_pooled.shape[0], 1).to(clip_pooled)

        force_zero_negative_prompt = is_negative_prompt and all(x == '' for x in prompt)

        if force_zero_negative_prompt:
            clip_pooled = torch.zeros_like(clip_pooled)
            cond_l = torch.zeros_like(cond_l)
            cond_g = torch.zeros_like(cond_g)

        cond = dict(
            crossattn=torch.cat([cond_l, cond_g], dim=2),
            vector=torch.cat([clip_pooled, flat], dim=1),
        )

        return cond

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt):
        _, token_count = self.text_processing_engine_l.process_texts([prompt])
        return token_count, self.text_processing_engine_l.get_target_prompt_token_count(token_count)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def encode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.encode(x.movedim(1, -1) * 0.5 + 0.5)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_in(sample)
        return sample.to(x)

    @torch.inference_mode()
    def decode_first_stage(self, x):
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.first_stage_model.process_out(x)
        sample = self.forge_objects.vae.decode(sample).movedim(-1, 1) * 2.0 - 1.0
        return sample.to(x)

- The class StableDiffusionXL inherits from ForgeDiffusionEngine and declares a patch-target via matched_guesses.

- It creates two separate text processing engines:
  - text_processing_engine_l for the left/text-encoder path with embedding projection disabled.
  - text_processing_engine_g for the right/second text-encoder path with embedding projection enabled and pooling.
- An embedder (Timestep) is used to generate additional conditioning tokens based on image size.
- ForgeObjects aggregates the patched components (unet, clip, vae); there are copies for original and post-LoRA states.
- Several helper methods expose standard diffusion engine hooks: conditioning retrieval, prompt length querying, and first-stage encoding/decoding.

- StableDiffusionXL — class
  - Description: Main diffusion engine for SDXL integrating patched components (CLIP, VAE, Unet) and dual text-processing pathways for conditioning.
  - Importance: Entrypoint that wires patching and processing pipelines to produce conditioning, prompt handling, and stage I/O.

- __init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components)
  - Description: Initializes patching and processing components for SDXL.
  - Importance: Builds CLIP patcher with two encoders/tokenizers, VAE patch, Unet patch, two ClassicTextProcessingEngine instances (l and g), an embedder, and ForgeObjects. Sets a flag is_sdxl for legacy WebUI compatibility.

  - Description: Sets the clip_skip parameter on both left and right text processing engines.
  - Importance: Allows external control of CLIP skip behavior to influence text conditioning.

  - Description: Produces conditioning for the model given a prompt by combining left and right text encodings and CLIP features.
  - Importance: Core method for building the cross-attention and vector conditioning inputs used during diffusion steps. It:
    - Ensures the patcher is resident on the GPU.
    - Runs both text_processing_engine_l and text_processing_engine_g to obtain cond_l and cond_g, as well as clip_pooled.
    - Extracts image dimensions from the prompt and creates additional positional conditioning via embedder.
    - Handles a special case for forced-zero negative prompts.

  - CLIP (via CLIP patcher) with two text encoders/tokenizers (clip_l and clip_g).
  - VAE (via VAE patcher).
  - Unet (via UnetPatcher.from_model) integrating a diffusers scheduler.
- It creates two separate text processing engines:
  - text_processing_engine_l for the left/text-encoder path with embedding projection disabled.
  - text_processing_engine_g for the right/second text-encoder path with embedding projection enabled and pooling.
- An embedder (Timestep) is used to generate additional conditioning tokens based on image size.
- ForgeObjects aggregates the patched components (unet, clip, vae); there are copies for original and post-LoRA states.
- Several helper methods expose standard diffusion engine hooks: conditioning retrieval, prompt length querying, and first-stage encoding/decoding.

### Functions & Classes
- StableDiffusionXL — class
  - Description: Main diffusion engine for SDXL integrating patched components (CLIP, VAE, Unet) and dual text-processing pathways for conditioning.
  - Importance: Entrypoint that wires patching and processing pipelines to produce conditioning, prompt handling, and stage I/O.

- __init__(self, estimated_config, huggingface_components)
  - Description: Initializes patching and processing components for SDXL.
  - Importance: Builds CLIP patcher with two encoders/tokenizers, VAE patch, Unet patch, two ClassicTextProcessingEngine instances (l and g), an embedder, and ForgeObjects. Sets a flag is_sdxl for legacy WebUI compatibility.

- set_clip_skip(self, clip_skip)
  - Description: Sets the clip_skip parameter on both left and right text processing engines.
  - Importance: Allows external control of CLIP skip behavior to influence text conditioning.

- get_learned_conditioning(self, prompt: list[str])
  - Description: Produces conditioning for the model given a prompt by combining left and right text encodings and CLIP features.
  - Importance: Core method for building the cross-attention and vector conditioning inputs used during diffusion steps. It:
    - Ensures the patcher is resident on the GPU.
    - Runs both text_processing_engine_l and text_processing_engine_g to obtain cond_l and cond_g, as well as clip_pooled.
    - Extracts image dimensions from the prompt and creates additional positional conditioning via embedder.
    - Handles a special case for forced-zero negative prompts.
    - Returns a conditioning dictionary with crossattn and vector components.

- get_prompt_lengths_on_ui(self, prompt)
  - Description: Returns token counts for a given prompt as used by the UI.
  - Importance: Supports UI display/validation by exposing token lengths relevant to the left-text path.

- encode_first_stage(self, x)
  - Description: Encodes input images through the VAE’s first-stage pipeline to latent representations.
  - Importance: Provides the forward pass needed for creating latent codes before diffusion.

- decode_first_stage(self, x)
  - Description: Decodes latent representations back into image space via the VAE’s first-stage model.
  - Importance: Provides the reverse pass to recover images from latents after diffusion.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This file is central to patching and Florence-like integration because:
  - It explicitly constructs patched components: CLIP, VAE, and Unet via UnetPatcher, which are core targets for patch hooks.
  - It exposes two text-processing pipelines (text_processing_engine_l and text_processing_engine_g) that are critical for applying or testing patch-based modifications to conditioning.
  - The get_learned_conditioning method builds conditioning in a way that combines patched CLIP outputs with patchable conditioning vectors, making it a focal point for patch-aware adjustments.
  - The presence of ForgeObjects and the explicit patch-oriented initialization (VAE patch, Unet patch, CLIP patch) aligns with a Florence-like patching workflow, including GPU memory handling (memory_management.load_model_gpu) and patch state copies (forge_objects_original, forge_objects_after_applying_lora).
- In summary, this file contains the key hooks, wiring, and conditioning logic that a code-mod agent would examine to identify patch hooks, integration points for Florence, and areas to modify or extend for patch-based experimentation.

If I do not update this "Forge Status" then it means I cannot reproduce any problem. In that case, fresh re-install should help most. # UnetPatcher Below are self-supported **single file** of all codes to implement FreeU V2. See also `extension-builtin/sd_forge_freeu/scripts/forge_freeu.py`: ```python import torch import gradio as gr from modules import scripts def Fourier_filter(x, threshold, scale): # FFT x_freq = torch.fft.fftn(x.float(), dim=(-2, -1)) x_freq = torch.fft.fftshift(x_freq, dim=(-2, -1)) B, C, H, W = x_freq.shape mask = torch.ones((B, C, H, W), device=x.device) crow, ccol = H // 2, W // 2 mask[..., crow - threshold:crow + threshold, ccol - threshold:ccol + threshold] = scale x_freq = x_freq * mask # IFFT x_freq = torch.fft.ifftshift(x_freq, dim=(-2, -1)) x_filtered = torch.fft.ifftn(x_freq, dim=(-2, -1)).real return x_filtered.to(x.dtype) def patch_freeu_v2(unet_patcher, b1, b2, s1, s2): model_channels = unet_patcher.model.diffusion_model.config["model_channels"] scale_dict = {model_channels * 4: (b1, s1), model_channels * 2: (b2, s2)} on_cpu_devices = {} def output_block_patch(h, hsp, transformer_options): scale = scale_dict.get(h.shape[1], None) if scale is not None: hidden_mean = h.mean(1).unsqueeze(1) B = hidden_mean.shape[0] hidden_max, _ = torch.max(hidden_mean.view(B, -1), dim=-1, keepdim=True) hidden_min, _ = torch.min(hidden_mean.view(B, -1), dim=-1, keepdim=True) hidden_mean = (hidden_mean - hidden_min.unsqueeze(2).unsqueeze(3)) / (hidden_max - hidden_min).unsqueeze(2).unsqueeze(3) h[:, :h.shape[1] // 2] = h[:, :h.shape[1] // 2] * ((scale[0] - 1) * hidden_mean + 1) if hsp.device not in on_cpu_devices: try: hsp = Fourier_filter(hsp, threshold=1, scale=scale[1]) except: print("Device", hsp.device, "does not support the torch.fft.") on_cpu_devices[hsp.device] = True hsp = Fourier_filter(hsp.cpu(), threshold=1, scale=scale[1]).to(hsp.device) else: hsp = Fourier_filter(hsp.cpu(), threshold=1, scale=scale[1]).to(hsp.device) return h, hsp m = unet_patcher.clone() m.set_model_output_block_patch(output_block_patch) return m class FreeUForForge(scripts.Script): sorting_priority = 12 # It will be the 12th item on UI. def title(self): return "FreeU Integrated" def show(self, is_img2img): # make this extension visible in both txt2img and img2img tab. return scripts.AlwaysVisible def ui(self, *args, **kwargs): with gr.Accordion(open=False, label=self.title()): freeu_enabled = gr.Checkbox(label='Enabled', value=False) freeu_b1 = gr.Slider(label='B1', minimum=0, maximum=2, step=0.01, value=1.01) freeu_b2 = gr.Slider(label='B2', minimum=0, maximum=2, step=0.01, value=1.02) freeu_s1 = gr.Slider(label='S1', minimum=0, maximum=4, step=0.01, value=0.99) freeu_s2 = gr.Slider(label='S2', minimum=0, maximum=4, step=0.01, value=0.95) return freeu_enabled, freeu_b1, freeu_b2, freeu_s1, freeu_s2 def process_before_every_sampling(self, p, *script_args, **kwargs): # This will be called before every sampling. # If you use highres fix, this will be called twice. freeu_enabled, freeu_b1, freeu_b2, freeu_s1, freeu_s2 = script_args if not freeu_enabled: return unet = p.sd_model.forge_objects.unet unet = patch_freeu_v2(unet, freeu_b1, freeu_b2, freeu_s1, freeu_s2) p.sd_model.forge_objects.unet = unet # Below codes will add some logs to the texts below the image outputs on UI. # The extra_generation_params does not influence results. p.extra_generation_params.update(dict( freeu_enabled=freeu_enabled, freeu_b1=freeu_b1, freeu_b2=freeu_b2, freeu_s1=freeu_s1, freeu_s2=freeu_s2, )) return ``` See also [Forge's Unet Implementation](https://github.com/lllyasviel/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/blob/main/backend/nn/unet.py). # Under Construction WebUI Forge is now under some constructions, and docs / UI / functionality may change with updates.

### File Overview
This README.md provides a high-level overview of the Stable Diffusion WebUI Forge project and, in particular, documents the UnetPatcher approach for integrating FreeU V2 patching. It includes a code snippet showing the core functions and a class (FreeUForForge) that hooks patching into the Forge UI.

### Architecture
- The document is organized into project context (Forge overview, installation, status) and a focused technical section titled UnetPatcher.
- The UnetPatcher section presents a self-contained set of Python components intended to patch the UNet model before sampling:
  - Fourier_filter and patch_freeu_v2 implement the core patching algorithm.
  - FreeUForForge is a Script subclass that wires the patch into the Forge UI, exposes configuration controls, and applies the patch before each sampling.
- The patching workflow is designed to clone and modify the UNet, set a patch function for the model's output blocks, and store configuration in extra_generation_params for UI visibility/logging.

### Functions & Classes
- Fourier_filter — Description: Applies a frequency-domain filter to x using a FFT, a mask determined by threshold, and a scaling factor. It performs FFT, shifts, masks a central region, applies the mask, and then IFFT to return a filtered tensor. Importance: This is the core utility used by the patching routine to affect spatial-frequency components of certain activations (hsp) during patching.

- patch_freeu_v2 — Description: Constructs a patched UNet patcher from an existing unet_patcher. It uses model channel size to map scale parameters (b1/s1 and b2/s2) to specific channel blocks and defines an inner function output_block_patch(h, hsp, transformer_options) that applies per-block scaling and, when applicable, applies Fourier_filter to hsp. It clones the provided unet_patcher, assigns the patch function via set_model_output_block_patch, and returns the modified patcher m. Importance: This is the central function that implements the FreeU V2 patch logic and wires it into the Forge UNet patching mechanism.

- FreeUForForge — Description: A class that inherits from scripts.Script and integrates the FreeU V2 patch into the Forge UI. Key elements:
  - sorting_priority — Value: 12. Indicates UI positioning (12th item).
  - title(self) — Returns "FreeU Integrated", the display name in the UI.
  - show(self, is_img2img) — AlwaysVisible, ensuring the extension appears in both txt2img and img2img tabs.
  - ui(self, *args, **kwargs) — Builds an accordion UI with controls: Enabled, B1, B2, S1, S2, returning those controls for wiring into processing.
  - process_before_every_sampling(self, p, *script_args, **kwargs) — Called before every sampling; if enabled, it retrieves the UNet from p.sd_model.forge_objects.unet, applies patch_freeu_v2, updates p.sd_model.forge_objects.unet, and records the parameters into p.extra_generation_params for logging/traceability. Importance: This class is the integration point between the patch logic and the WebUI Forge script lifecycle, enabling user-configurable patching to run automatically during generation.

- Inner mechanics (within patch_freeu_v2) — Description: Defines output_block_patch(h, hsp, transformer_options), which:
  - Determines a scale based on the block’s channel dimension.
  - Normalizes a hidden_mean vector and uses it to modulate the first half of the channels.
  - Applies Fourier_filter to hsp for certain devices, with device-caching logic to gracefully fall back to CPU when FFT is unsupported.
  - Returns the potentially patched h and hsp.
  Importance: This inner function is the per-block patching procedure that enacts the FreeU V2 modifications during UNet block processing.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The agent is interested in Florence patch references and patching conventions. This README’s UnetPatcher section explicitly documents a patching convention and concrete implementation for integrating FreeU V2 into the SD WebUI Forge ecosystem.
- It exposes how patching is targeted at UNet blocks, how per-block scaling and frequency-domain filtering are applied, and how patches are wired into the Forge Script/UI lifecycle.
- It also shows how configuration (B1, B2, S1, S2) is exposed to users and persisted in extra_generation_params, which is useful for understanding how patch settings are communicated to the generation process and UI for reproducibility.

IFFT x_freq = torch.fft.ifftshift(x_freq, dim=(-2, -1)) x_filtered = torch.fft.ifftn(x_freq, dim=(-2, -1)).real return x_filtered.to(x.dtype) def patch_freeu_v2(unet_patcher, b1, b2, s1, s2): model_channels = unet_patcher.model.diffusion_model.config["model_channels"] scale_dict = {model_channels * 4: (b1, s1), model_channels * 2: (b2, s2)} on_cpu_devices = {} def output_block_patch(h, hsp, transformer_options): scale = scale_dict.get(h.shape[1], None) if scale is not None: hidden_mean = h.mean(1).unsqueeze(1) B = hidden_mean.shape[0] hidden_max, _ = torch.max(hidden_mean.view(B, -1), dim=-1, keepdim=True) hidden_min, _ = torch.min(hidden_mean.view(B, -1), dim=-1, keepdim=True) hidden_mean = (hidden_mean - hidden_min.unsqueeze(2).unsqueeze(3)) / (hidden_max - hidden_min).unsqueeze(2).unsqueeze(3) h[:, :h.shape[1] // 2] = h[:, :h.shape[1] // 2] * ((scale[0] - 1) * hidden_mean + 1) if hsp.device not in on_cpu_devices: try: hsp = Fourier_filter(hsp, threshold=1, scale=scale[1]) except: print("Device", hsp.device, "does not support the torch.fft.") on_cpu_devices[hsp.device] = True hsp = Fourier_filter(hsp.cpu(), threshold=1, scale=scale[1]).to(hsp.device) else: hsp = Fourier_filter(hsp.cpu(), threshold=1, scale=scale[1]).to(hsp.device) return h, hsp m = unet_patcher.clone() m.set_model_output_block_patch(output_block_patch) return m class FreeUForForge(scripts.Script): sorting_priority = 12 # It will be the 12th item on UI. def title(self): return "FreeU Integrated" def show(self, is_img2img): # make this extension visible in both txt2img and img2img tab. return scripts.AlwaysVisible def ui(self, *args, **kwargs): with gr.Accordion(open=False, label=self.title()): freeu_enabled = gr.Checkbox(label='Enabled', value=False) freeu_b1 = gr.Slider(label='B1', minimum=0, maximum=2, step=0.01, value=1.01) freeu_b2 = gr.Slider(label='B2', minimum=0, maximum=2, step=0.01, value=1.02) freeu_s1 = gr.Slider(label='S1', minimum=0, maximum=4, step=0.01, value=0.99) freeu_s2 = gr.Slider(label='S2', minimum=0, maximum=4, step=0.01, value=0.95) return freeu_enabled, freeu_b1, freeu_b2, freeu_s1, freeu_s2 def process_before_every_sampling(self, p, *script_args, **kwargs): # ### File Overview
This README.md provides a high-level overview of the Stable Diffusion WebUI Forge project and, in particular, documents the UnetPatcher approach for integrating FreeU V2 patching. It includes a code snippet showing the core functions and a class (FreeUForForge) that hooks patching into the Forge UI.

### Architecture
- The document is organized into project context (Forge overview, installation, status) and a focused technical section titled UnetPatcher.
- The UnetPatcher section presents a self-contained set of Python components intended to patch the UNet model before sampling:
  - Fourier_filter and patch_freeu_v2 implement the core patching algorithm.
  - FreeUForForge is a Script subclass that wires the patch into the Forge UI, exposes configuration controls, and applies the patch before each sampling.
- The patching workflow is designed to clone and modify the UNet, set a patch function for the model's output blocks, and store configuration in extra_generation_params for UI visibility/logging.

### Functions & Classes
- Fourier_filter — Description: Applies a frequency-domain filter to x using a FFT, a mask determined by threshold, and a scaling factor. It performs FFT, shifts, masks a central region, applies the mask, and then IFFT to return a filtered tensor. Importance: This is the core utility used by the patching routine to affect spatial-frequency components of certain activations (hsp) during patching.

- patch_freeu_v2 — Description: Constructs a patched UNet patcher from an existing unet_patcher. It uses model channel size to map scale parameters (b1/s1 and b2/s2) to specific channel blocks and defines an inner function output_block_patch(h, hsp, transformer_options) that applies per-block scaling and, when applicable, applies Fourier_filter to hsp. It clones the provided unet_patcher, assigns the patch function via set_model_output_block_patch, and returns the modified patcher m. Importance: This is the central function that implements the FreeU V2 patch logic and wires it into the Forge UNet patching mechanism.

- FreeUForForge — Description: A class that inherits from scripts.Script and integrates the FreeU V2 patch into the Forge UI. Key elements:
  - sorting_priority — Value: 12. Indicates UI positioning (12th item).
  - title(self) — Returns "FreeU Integrated", the display name in the UI.
  - show(self, is_img2img) — AlwaysVisible, ensuring the extension appears in both txt2img and img2img tabs.
  - ui(self, *args, **kwargs) — Builds an accordion UI with controls: Enabled, B1, B2, S1, S2, returning those controls for wiring into processing.
  - process_before_every_sampling(self, p, *script_args, **kwargs) — Called before every sampling; if enabled, it retrieves the UNet from p.sd_model.forge_objects.unet, applies patch_freeu_v2, updates p.sd_model.forge_objects.unet, and records the parameters into p.extra_generation_params for logging/traceability. Importance: This class is the integration point between the patch logic and the WebUI Forge script lifecycle, enabling user-configurable patching to run automatically during generation.

- Inner mechanics (within patch_freeu_v2) — Description: Defines output_block_patch(h, hsp, transformer_options), which:
  - Determines a scale based on the block’s channel dimension.
  - Normalizes a hidden_mean vector and uses it to modulate the first half of the channels.
  - Applies Fourier_filter to hsp for certain devices, with device-caching logic to gracefully fall back to CPU when FFT is unsupported.
  - Returns the potentially patched h and hsp.
  Importance: This inner function is the per-block patching procedure that enacts the FreeU V2 modifications during UNet block processing.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The agent is interested in Florence patch references and patching conventions. This README’s UnetPatcher section explicitly documents a patching convention and concrete implementation for integrating FreeU V2 into the SD WebUI Forge ecosystem.
- It exposes how patching is targeted at UNet blocks, how per-block scaling and frequency-domain filtering are applied, and how patches are wired into the Forge Script/UI lifecycle.
- It also shows how configuration (B1, B2, S1, S2) is exposed to users and persisted in extra_generation_params, which is useful for understanding how patch settings are communicated to the generation process and UI for reproducibility.

import torch
from backend import operations, memory_management
from backend.patcher.base import ModelPatcher

from transformers import modeling_utils


class DiffusersModelPatcher:
    def __init__(self, pipeline_class, dtype=torch.float16, *args, **kwargs):
        load_device = memory_management.get_torch_device()
        offload_device = torch.device("cpu")

        if not memory_management.should_use_fp16(device=load_device):
            dtype = torch.float32

        self.dtype = dtype

        with operations.using_forge_operations():
            with modeling_utils.no_init_weights():
                self.pipeline = pipeline_class.from_pretrained(*args, **kwargs)

        if hasattr(self.pipeline, 'unet'):
            if hasattr(self.pipeline.unet, 'set_attn_processor'):
                from diffusers.models.attention_processor import AttnProcessor2_0
                self.pipeline.unet.set_attn_processor(AttnProcessor2_0())
                print('Attention optimization applied to DiffusersModelPatcher')

        self.pipeline = self.pipeline.to(device=offload_device)

        if self.dtype == torch.float16:
            self.pipeline = self.pipeline.half()

        self.pipeline.eval()

        self.patcher = ModelPatcher(
            model=self.pipeline,
            load_device=load_device,
            offload_device=offload_device)

    def prepare_memory_before_sampling(self, batchsize, latent_width, latent_height):
        area = 2 * batchsize * latent_width * latent_height
        inference_memory = (((area * 0.6) / 0.9) + 1024) * (1024 * 1024)
        memory_management.load_models_gpu(
            models=[self.patcher],
            memory_required=inference_memory
        )

    def move_tensor_to_current_device(self, x):
        return x.to(device=self.patcher.current_device, dtype=self.dtype)
